title: 10 best herbal essential oils for difficulty chewing
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for difficulty chewing are natural remedies that alleviate discomfort and pain associated with chewing difficulties, making it easier to consume food and maintain a healthy diet. The best herbal essential oils for this issue are Zingiber officinale, Cinnamomum verum, Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Rosmarinus officinalis, Salvia officinalis, Foeniculum vulgare, Thymus vulgaris, Syzygium aromaticum, and Eucalyptus globulus. If left untreated, difficulty chewing can lead to malnutrition, social isolation, and a decreased quality of life, affecting daily activities and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. It contains a compound called glycyrretinic acid, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and throat, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, licorice oil has natural antibacterial properties that can help to combat infections and promote healing in the gums and oral mucosa, further alleviating difficulty chewing. Overall, licorice essential oils provides a natural and effective solution for those experiencing difficulties with chewing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw, mouth, and gums, making it easier to chew food.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of herbal licorice essential oil help protect against free radicals that can damage oral tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anesthetic and analgesic: Herbal licorice essential oil's anesthetic and analgesic properties numb the pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing, making it easier to eat and maintain a balanced diet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in alleviating gum inflammation and pain associated with difficulty chewing.
Barks: Licorice bark is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots weigh 500 grams and chop into small pieces.
Dry the chopped glycyrrhiza glabra root fragments in a warm oven at 60 degrees celsius for eight hours.
Steam distill the dried glycyrrhiza glabra root material using a clevenger apparatus with distilled water and collect essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final herbal glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in dark glass vials at room temperature for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its calming and relaxing properties that alleviate muscle tension in the jaw, face, and mouth. The oil's antispasmodic effects help to reduce involuntary contractions that can make it difficult to chew or swallow, while its soothing qualities ease discomfort and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, lemon balm essential oils provide a natural solution for individuals struggling with difficulty chewing.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw, mouth, and gums, making it easier to chew and swallow.

Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Properties: Lemon balm essential oil's antioxidant properties can protect against oral infections and inflammation, while its antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria and viruses that may be contributing to difficulty chewing.

Calming and Sedative Effects: The calming properties of lemon balm essential oil can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common factors in tension headaches and jaw pain that can make eating and chewing difficult.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.
Flowers: The flowers of the lemon balm plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to relax muscle spasms and ease jaw tension, alleviating difficulties in chewing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a pesticide-free area or purchase dried herbs.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 24-48 hours.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves using a still or a professional essential oil distillation kit.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles within 30 minutes.
Store the finished melissa officinalis essential oil in a cool, dark place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, peppermint oil can help ease tension in the jaw muscles and reduce pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can provide a soothing relief for sore teeth and gums, making it easier to chew and swallow food. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals experiencing chronic mouth and facial pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain associated with conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, which can cause difficulty chewing.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of peppermint essential oil can help alleviate toothache pain and gum inflammation, making it easier to chew food without discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market in the morning.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 500 ml of distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillation mixture and decant it into a dark glass bottle immediately after collection.
Store the mentha x piperita essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

Mix the extracted essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a 10% dilution ratio for topical application.

Steam distill the crushed foeniculum vulgare using a distillation apparatus at 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the foeniculum vulgare essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat can alleviate swelling and pain associated with conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and gum disease. It also has antibacterial properties that can combat oral infections and promote healthy gum tissue. By addressing these underlying issues, Thymus vulgaris essential oils can help improve chewing function and overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, making it easier to chew and swallow.
Carvacrol: As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, carvacrol can help soothe oral inflammation and discomfort, allowing for smoother and more comfortable chewing.
Bornyl acetate: This sesquiterpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the mouth and jaw, making chewing and eating more manageable.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used due to their high thymol content, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with difficulty chewing.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used due to their thymol and linalool content, which helps to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and ease pain in the jaw and mouth, making it easier to chew.

Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are used due to their high concentration of thymol and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce pain, inflammation, and swelling in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at a temperature of 100-150c for 2-3 hours to release oils.
Collect the distilled oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities immediately.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 24-48 hours before using or bottling it for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because it possesses anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the jaw and mouth. The oil's warm, spicy aroma can also stimulate the senses and promote relaxation, making it easier to focus on eating and swallowing. Additionally, its natural antimicrobial properties may help prevent infections that can cause sore throats and mouth ulcers, further contributing to improved oral comfort.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Helps with difficulty chewing by reducing inflammation and pain in the gums and jaw area, making it easier to chew and swallow.
Cinnamaldehyde: Relaxes the muscles and reduces spasms in the jaw, making it easier to chew and swallow food.
Gallic acid: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew and eat.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used to make essential oils because they contain a high concentration of Eugenol, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the mouth and jaw.
Leaves: They are used to make essential oils because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the mouth and jaw.

Flowers: They are used to make essential oils because they contain Eugenol and other compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate pain and discomfort in the mouth and jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of syzygium aromaticum flowers at peak ripeness when they are fully open and fragrant.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried flowers for 4-6 hours using a ratio of 1:2 with distilled water.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the hydrosol through decantation and discard the solids.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 25c for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. When inhaled, it helps to clear nasal passages and relieve sinus pressure, making breathing and chewing easier. The oil's expectorant properties also help to thin mucus, making it less likely to obstruct the airways and cause discomfort while chewing. Additionally, eucalyptus oil's analgesic properties can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing, promoting a smoother and more comfortable eating experience.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with difficulty chewing by reducing inflammation and pain in the mouth and jaw, allowing for smoother and more comfortable chewing.
Piperitone: Assists with difficulty chewing by providing a calming effect, reducing anxiety and stress, which can contribute to jaw tension and difficulty chewing.
α-Pinene: Contributes to relief from difficulty chewing by acting as an expectorant, helping to clear out mucus and debris from the mouth and throat, making chewing and swallowing easier.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound that provides relief from pain and inflammation associated with difficulty chewing.

Buds: The buds are also used for essential oil production, as they contain a higher concentration of eucalyptol than mature leaves and are often used in combination with leaves for a more potent effect.

Barks: The barks are used for essential oil production, particularly for their high concentration of eucalyptol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with difficulty chewing.
